response:
To compute the **sample standard deviation** of the data set:

$$
\{-11, 9, 5, 15, -10, -15, 12, -25, -22\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the mean (average) of the data set

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{-11 + 9 + 5 + 15 - 10 - 15 + 12 - 25 - 22}{9}
$$

$$
= \frac{(-11 - 10 - 15 - 25 - 22) + (9 + 5 + 15 + 12)}{9}
$$

$$
= \frac{(-73) + (41)}{9} = \frac{-32}{9}
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the squared differences from the mean

We calculate the squared difference for each data point:

$$
\begin{align*}
(-11 - (-32/9))^2 &= \left(-11 + \frac{32}{9}\right)^2 = \left(-\frac{99}{9} + \frac{32}{9}\right)^2 = \left(-\frac{67}{9}\right)^2 = \frac{4489}{81} \\
9 - (-32/9) &= \frac{81}{9} + \frac{32}{9} = \frac{113}{9} \\
\left(\frac{113}{9}\right)^2 &= \frac{12769}{81} \\
5 - (-32/9) &= \frac{45}{9} + \frac{32}{9} = \frac{77}{9} \\
\left(\frac{77}{9}\right)^2 &= \frac{5929}{81} \\
15 - (-32/9) &= \frac{135}{9} + \frac{32}{9} = \frac{167}{9} \\
\left(\frac{167}{9}\right)^2 &= \frac{27889}{81} \\
